title: DORA: Lead Time for Changes
description: DORA Pillar: Lead Time for Changes — Measures the time from code commit to production deployment. Shorter lead times indicate faster feedback loops, better automation, and quicker value delivery. Includes PR cycle time as the primary measurable component.

list.name: environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.label: Environment

list.name: copilot_cohort
list.query: All, Copilot Active, Copilot Inactive, Agent Users, Non-Agent Users
list.label: Copilot Cohort

list.name: Deployments
list.rawQuery:
SELECT d.created_at AS time, 'Deploy: ' || d.sha AS text, 'Deployments' AS tags
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

options.content:
# ⏱ Lead Time for Changes

**DORA Pillar: Throughput**

## What it Measures
Lead Time for Changes measures the time it takes for a code commit to reach production. This includes the time for code review (PR cycle time) and the deployment pipeline. It is the second DORA *throughput* metric and reflects how quickly your team can deliver value once code is written.

## Why it Matters
Shorter lead times mean faster feedback loops, reduced risk per change, and quicker delivery of features and fixes to users. Long lead times often indicate bottlenecks in code review, testing, or deployment processes.

## How to Interpret
| Performance Level | Lead Time | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| 🟢 Elite | Less than 1 hour | Automated pipelines, fast reviews, small changes |
| 🟡 High | 1 hour to 1 day | Good automation with some review delays |
| 🟠 Medium | 1 day to 1 week | Manual steps or batch processing slowing delivery |
| 🔴 Low | More than 1 week | Significant bottlenecks in review or deployment |

**PR Cycle Time** (created → merged) is the largest measurable component of lead time.

## How to Take Action
- **If high lead time**: Look for bottlenecks in code review (long PR wait times), reduce PR size, automate testing
- **If PR cycle time is high**: Add more reviewers, set review SLAs, use Copilot Code Review for faster initial feedback
- **If trending up**: Check for team capacity issues, increasing PR complexity, or CI pipeline slowdowns

panels.title: 📊 Key Metrics — Current lead time performance
⏱ Change Lead Time
panels.description:
DORA: Throughput · Median time from PR merge to successful deployment

🟢 Elite: <1 hr  ·  🟡 High: <1 day  ·  🟠 Medium: <1 week  ·  🔴 Low: >1 week

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600
  ) AS median_lead_time_hours
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.id = dpr.pull_request_id
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
  AND (
    '$copilot_cohort' = 'All'
    OR ('$copilot_cohort' = 'Copilot Active' AND pr.author_user_id IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM copilot_user_activity
      WHERE is_active = true AND activity_date BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
    ))
    OR ('$copilot_cohort' = 'Copilot Inactive' AND pr.author_user_id NOT IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM copilot_user_activity
      WHERE is_active = true AND activity_date BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
    ))
  )

panels.title: 🔁 PR Cycle Time
panels.description:
Supplementary · Median time from PR creation to merge

🟢 Fast: <4 hrs  ·  🟡 Normal: <24 hrs  ·  🟠 Slow: <72 hrs  ·  🔴 Very Slow: >72 hrs

targets.rawSql:
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600
) AS median_cycle_time_hours
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
  AND (
    '$copilot_cohort' = 'All'
    OR ('$copilot_cohort' = 'Copilot Active' AND pr.author_user_id IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM copilot_user_activity
      WHERE is_active = true AND activity_date BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
    ))
    OR ('$copilot_cohort' = 'Copilot Inactive' AND pr.author_user_id NOT IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM copilot_user_activity
      WHERE is_active = true AND activity_date BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
    ))
  )

panels.title: 📈 Change Lead Time Trend
panels.description:
DORA: Throughput · Weekly median change lead time in hours
Lower is better — Elite teams deliver in under 1 hour

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600
  ) AS median_lead_time_hours
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.id = dpr.pull_request_id
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
  AND (
    '$copilot_cohort' = 'All'
    OR ('$copilot_cohort' = 'Copilot Active' AND pr.author_user_id IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM copilot_user_activity
      WHERE is_active = true AND activity_date BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
    ))
    OR ('$copilot_cohort' = 'Copilot Inactive' AND pr.author_user_id NOT IN (
      SELECT user_id FROM copilot_user_activity
      WHERE is_active = true AND activity_date BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
    ))
  )
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: 📄 Recent PRs
panels.description: Recently merged pull requests with cycle time

targets.rawSql:
SELECT pr.number, pr.title, u.login AS author, pr.created_at, pr.merged_at,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600 AS cycle_time_hours,
  pr.additions, pr.deletions
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = pr.author_user_id
WHERE pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY pr.merged_at DESC
LIMIT 20
